I'm glad that for you, the central message was not specifically about Mormonism. But for many other people, it was. Including this article.

This article says:

>Mormonism *does* have a pattern of violence and patriarchal fundamentalism, regardless of our protestations otherwise. Not every Mormon is violent, but every Mormon has participated in a system of violence whenever upholding doctrines of racism, sexism, and homophobia. >  
>Ignoring the dark aspects of our past, culture, and doctrine means we drink from what Anne Michaels calls the "poisoned well" of history, "seeping into the ground-water." Which is precisely why Mormonism continues to be the root of atrocities like the Lafferty murders, the Daybell murders, Samuel Shaffer and the Knights of the Crystal Blade, Susan Powell's disappearance, and Elizabeth Smart's kidnapping.
